steps.title: Identify what the conclusion requires
steps.content: The team concluded that the ability to distinguish spontaneous from volitional laughter is universal across cultures. The correct finding must show (1) participants could distinguish the laugh types above chance, and (2) this held equally across societies.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — participants' evaluations correlated with economic conditions of societies: Ties recognition to economic variables — a factor that varies by culture — which would undermine a universal claim. ✗
  • Choice B — 64% correct overall, and recognition rates similar across Austria, Samoa, and all societies: Shows above-chance accuracy (supporting the ability to distinguish) and similar rates across societies (supporting universality). ✓
  • Choice C — acoustic features of spontaneous laughter vary within and across societies: Describes properties of the laughs themselves, not whether listeners across cultures can correctly identify them. ✗
  • Choice D — no significant differences in listeners' judgments between Austria and Samoa only: Limited to just two societies; does not speak to the other 19 societies needed to claim a universal finding. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
